title: podman build for non-host platform stopped working after upgrading
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On macOS, previously, I was able to build images for platforms other than my host platform; but, after upgrading to podman 4.3.0, this no longer works.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init --cpus 4 --memory 10240 --disk-size 100`
2. `podman machine start`
3. `podman system connection default podman-machine-default-root`
4. `podman machine ssh "sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static; sudo systemctl reboot"`
5. `git clone https://github.com/IBM/containerdiag`
6. `cd containerdiag`
7. `podman build --platform linux/ppc64le --jobs=1 --manifest localhost/containerdiag:latest .`

**Describe the results you received:**

For example, running with `--platform linux/ppc64le` on a host platform of `x86_64` shows that the build is using `x86_64` instead:

```
$ podman build --platform linux/ppc64le --jobs=1 --manifest localhost/containerdiag:latest .
[...]
Installing:
 binutils                     x86_64 2.37-36.fc36                 updates 6.0 M
```

However, using `podman run` works as expected:

```
$ for p in linux/amd64 linux/arm64/v8 linux/ppc64le linux/s390x; do podman run --rm --platform $p -it docker.io/fedora uname -m 2>/dev/null; done
x86_64
aarch64
ppc64le
s390x
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman build --platform` should build with the specified platform.

**Output of `podman version`:**

<details>
<summary>podman version</summary>

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Tue Oct 18 12:55:57 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Oct 21 03:16:35 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

</details>

comments.body:
I thought this was related to the `podman` version, but I downgraded to my previous version, and experienced the same issue. Then I realized that upgrading `podman` to 4.3.0 upgraded the version of CoreOS being used by `podman machine` (to an October build), even with the older `podman` version. I found I have some old images in `~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/` and created a `podman machine` with `--image-path` with one from May, and the problem was resolved:

1. `podman machine init --cpus 4 --memory 10240 --disk-size 100 --image-path ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz`
2. `podman machine start`
3. `podman system connection default podman-machine-default-root`
4. `podman machine ssh "sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static; sudo systemctl reboot"`
5. `git clone https://github.com/IBM/containerdiag`
6. `cd containerdiag`
7. `podman build --platform linux/ppc64le --jobs=1 --manifest localhost/containerdiag:latest .`
   ```
   [...]
    binutils                     ppc64le 2.37-36.fc36                updates 6.7 M
   ```

Is this an issue with CoreOS or podman?

Relatedly, if I didn't have the cached image, how would I have downloaded it? Where are they?

I found a list of "Stable" CoreOS builds here: https://builds.coreos.fedoraproject.org/browser?stream=stable&arch=x86_64

Then downloaded the "QEMU" builds, working backwards from the latest build to find where things stopped working, and the problem reproduced all the way back to 36.20220505.3.2 which is before the build that worked above. Maybe there's some sort of race condition when creating the image or running `rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static` that puts it into a bad state?

One more wrinkle: I can consistently reproduce the problem with the Stable CoreOS builds, and I can consistently not reproduce the problem using `~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz` which includes "dev" in the name that stable builds don't, so I'm not even sure where that image came from.

Looks like those dev images come from here: https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/testing/builds/
Nothing since May
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I don't believe these are supported, and we rely on fedora coreos for new machines now.
@baude Am I mistaken?

@kgibm There are packages that you need that aren't installed by default. This should fix you up:

```
sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static-aarch64 qemu-user-static-ppc qemu-user-static-s390x
sudo reboot
```

For rationale see:

- https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1088
- https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1237
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since you have a suggested solution, closing.
